summary: HRM-Text (GitHub Repo) HRM-Text is a 1B text generation model based on the HRM architecture. It can be trained with 130-600x less compute and 150-900x less data than foundation models, making foundation model pretraining accessible. The 0.6B parameter version of the model can be trained on 8 H100s on a single node in about 50 hours for around $800. The 1B parameter model can be trained on 16 H100s on two nodes in about 46 hours for around $1,472.

title: Rent the Intelligence. Own the Context
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-20, Reading time: 13 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2FBgiX5B/1/0100019e4549c8e1-667134ad-77dc-447f-ba07-2372670e47ab-000000/zM3Os-d0LvZIs7cLnR3WipzqGAyTXl0eNvwijyxUYgA=452
summary: Rent the Intelligence. Own the Context (13 minute read) Token control matters, but the bigger enterprise risk is letting the same vendor own the context layer. Models and agents will converge, but a company's working memory will not because it is made of promises, exceptions, scars, and decisions.

summary: CVE-2026-20182: Unauthenticated Cisco SD-WAN Control-Plane Compromise via vHub Authentication Bypass (11 minute read) Rapid7 researchers disclosed a CVSS 10.0 authentication bypass vulnerability in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN components. The flaw allows attackers to forge device authentication via a crafted DTLS session, potentially enabling persistent unauthorized access. Cisco recommends upgrading affected devices to specified firmware versions and reviewing logs for suspicious activity.

title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-20, Reading time: 4 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fkrebsonsecurity.com%2F2026%2F05%2Fcisa-admin-leaked-aws-govcloud-keys-on-github%2F%3Futm_source=tldrinfosec/1/0100019e457f6100-f041e421-7c79-4ab0-908b-c541cb593e60-000000/EL_Gk5n-nAiCAl4FpnCgxWNhMe4fc7cW_kbOt6agIrM=452
summary: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github (4 minute read) A CISA contractor used a public GitHub repo as a working scratchpad, exposing AWS GovCloud admin keys, plaintext passwords, and internal tooling details for months. GitGuardian flagged the leak, and researcher Philippe Caturegli confirmed the keys still worked for 48 hours after CISA was notified. Weak passwords and disabled GitHub secret-scanning point to basic hygiene and oversight failures at an already understaffed agency.

summary: Model half-life (4 minute read) Model half-life - the idea that model releases will keep getting faster and faster - doesn't really make sense under consideration. title: The Evolution of Cassandra Data Movement at Netflix
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-21, Reading time: 8 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2FYxD1OG/1/0100019e4a0319c8-748980c9-c1c6-4c23-8de8-bce0341f5604-000000/0J8CznD-an-dwRQbCJ1PQLO1g1IfcMCs_0FfTakGr7M=452
summary: The Evolution of Cassandra Data Movement at Netflix (8 minute read) Netflix replaced its Cassandra-to-Iceberg movement engine with a layered platform that reads backups directly from S3, converts them to Spark DataFrames, and lets each data abstraction build its own optimized connector. The engine moves about 3 PB/day, migration uses shadow validation, enhanced observability, and a Maestro Decider fallback to the prior solution, enabling a transparent cutover with zero downstream code changes.

title: mondayDB 3 – Solving HTAP for a Trillion-Table System
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-21, Reading time: 21 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fengineering.monday.com%2Fmondaydb-3-solving-htap-for-a-trillion-table-system%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019e4a0319c8-748980c9-c1c6-4c23-8de8-bce0341f5604-000000/UZGNm-rEYKUmdp1YK__DRH-lteCr4DHhSpy3ywdHi4I=452
summary: mondayDB 3 – Solving HTAP for a Trillion-Table System (21 minute read) mondayDB 3 is an HTAP system designed to handle over a trillion dynamic, constantly evolving tables with highly flexible schemas. They replaced their MySQL + JSON architecture with a CQRS-based Lambda architecture powered by DuckDB: immutable snapshots in object storage, an external WAL for real-time changes, and a soft-stateful serving layer that syncs + queries local DuckDB files on every read.

title: Returning to life!
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-21, Reading time: 6 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Ftidydesign.substack.com%2Fp%2Freturning-to-life%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019e4a0319c8-748980c9-c1c6-4c23-8de8-bce0341f5604-000000/Bib4n2Ms03BniNg2nBHqvr_VHa_8bxI48MVYy1HHbj0=452
summary: Returning to life! (6 minute read) AI is both genuinely empowering for data science. It makes programming, translation, voice input, and broad learning more accessible. However, it is genuinely harmful through environmental cost, copyright issues, wealth concentration, shallow thinking, and unequal access. The tension cannot be neatly resolved, but data science leaders still need to engage with AI seriously so they can help people use it well.

title: Better Experiments with LLM Evals — A funnel, not a fork

summary: Better Experiments with LLM Evals — A funnel, not a fork (4 minute read) Treat LLM evals and online A/B experiments as a funnel by using LLM judges early to verify quality (relevance, tone, and coherence) and filter out weak ideas before they consume experiment resources. This raises the success rate of experiments. Running evals on experiment results creates a feedback loop that continuously calibrates and improves the judges themselves.

title: Protocols for transactional usage of object storage
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-21, Reading time: 8 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.bitsxpages.com%2Fp%2Fprotocols-for-transactional-usage%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019e4a0319c8-748980c9-c1c6-4c23-8de8-bce0341f5604-000000/abg7gD6kbXXi6-t5j1s7PLa7ukQwLN-q4qVHkJaJf40=452
summary: Protocols for transactional usage of object storage (8 minute read) Object storage can support serializable OLTP if you build on three write primitives (atomic PUTs, conditional PUT If-Match/If-None-Match, and strongly consistent LISTs) and three read primitives (atomic GETs, conditional GET If-None-Match, and consistent listing). The core tradeoff is safety versus contention cost, and you need safe garbage collection to prevent the storage from growing indefinitely.

summary: How to Write "Garbage Code" (by Linus Torvalds) (7 minute read) Linus Torvalds, the creator of Git and Linux, says that overly complex or abstracted code, like unnecessary helper functions, makes code maintainability and understanding worse. It increases cognitive burden and, in some cases, it makes sense to repeat code to reduce human and LLM context switching. Code should be optimized for readability and ease of iteration.

comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-22, Reading time: 2 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fmediacat.uk%2Fdisguised-ai-ads-outperform-human-ones%2F%3Futm_source=tldrmarketing/1/0100019e4f5deb3f-b4d2ff86-7c07-45b5-aa7e-716a4e36f567-000000/n93ac6kVLbxlhZciQanr0VnBDhz92ILem-hguudGbM8=452
summary: Disguised AI ads outperform human ones (2 minute read) AI-generated ads match human performance overall, but those that look human-made drive the highest click-through rates. Most people misidentify AI images, often associating polished visuals and prominent faces with human work.

title: Making User-Sequence Data More Cost-Efficient, Faster, and Easier to Use
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-22, Reading time: 12 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2FMiBf5s/1/0100019e4f759df9-707169a6-e881-4a0a-90a1-e95fa2c06404-000000/HL7EERiZiaKqXjJnNdXThCuf6eKo-D8s5yNOlK3Wj9k=452
summary: Making User-Sequence Data More Cost-Efficient, Faster, and Easier to Use (12 minute read) Pinterest redesigned its machine learning data platform to use a single configuration-as-code definition that standardizes user-sequence data across real-time indexing, batch processing, and online serving. This lambda-style architecture and columnar, time-partitioned storage model reduced infrastructure costs while speeding up the onboarding of new events and enrichments.
